daily_brief: The day's most significant developments center on escalating conflict and humanitarian crises across multiple regions. Russia launched what Kyiv's mayor called the 'most massive attack' on the Ukrainian capital, killing at least 30 and injuring 91 in overnight drone and missile strikes [BBC, Al Jazeera]. Meanwhile, rescue efforts in Venezuela yielded a rare success as a survivor was pulled from rubble eight days after twin earthquakes that have now claimed at least 2,595 lives [BBC, Le Monde]. In France, authorities confirmed the June heatwave caused approximately 2,025 excess deaths—a 29% spike—with Paris seeing a 62% increase, prompting political fallout including a Green party no-confidence motion [France 24, RFI, The Hindu]. Geopolitically, NATO leaders prepare for next week's summit in Ankara amid transatlantic tensions over defense spending and US commitments [The Hindu], while China's expanded maritime activities east of Taiwan draw concern from Australia and signal a strategic shift in Beijing's approach to the contested waters [ABC Australia, Financial Times]. Iran prepares for Supreme Leader Khamenei's funeral, with representatives from over 100 countries expected to attend the seven-day commemoration [Al Jazeera, The Hindu].

top_stories.headline: Russia Launches 'Most Massive' Drone and Missile Attack on Kyiv, Killing at Least 30
bullets: Russian forces launched a combined drone and missile attack on Kyiv overnight Thursday, killing at least 30 people and injuring 91 in what Mayor Vitaly Klitschko called the 'most massive attack' on the capital
The attack deployed the largest number of weapons ever used against Kyiv, hitting multiple neighborhoods including a high-rise residential building and an ambulance station
Approximately 52,500 people, including 4,500 children, sheltered in metro stations overnight—the highest number in recent years
Moscow claimed it targeted military plants in retaliation for Ukrainian strikes on Russian civilian infrastructure; Ukraine accused Russia of deliberately targeting civilian areas

sources.name: BBC World
sources.framing_note: Emphasized scale of attack and civilian impact, included Kremlin justification

sources.name: Al Jazeera
sources.framing_note: Brief video coverage noting Ukrainian retaliatory strike on Belgorod

top_stories.headline: France Records Over 2,000 Excess Deaths During Record June Heatwave
bullets: Public Health France reported 2,025 additional deaths during the week of June 22-28, representing a 29.1% increase compared to the previous week
The Paris region saw deaths spike by 62% during the peak heatwave period, with similar increases in Pays de la Loire
Health Minister Stephanie Rist indicated the figure is likely an underestimate and the final tally remains incomplete
The Green party filed a no-confidence motion against the Lecornu government, accusing authorities of inadequate protective measures

sources.name: France 24
sources.framing_note: Focused on mortality statistics and government response

sources.name: RFI English
sources.framing_note: Compared French response to Spanish worker protection laws, discussed proposed climate leave legislation

sources.name: The Hindu
sources.framing_note: Straightforward coverage of statistics, noted comparison to 2003 heatwave deaths

top_stories.headline: Venezuela Earthquake Death Toll Reaches 2,595 as Survivor Rescued After Eight Days
bullets: Hernán Gil was rescued alive after being trapped for eight days under 140 tonnes of rubble following twin earthquakes on June 24
Venezuelan Acting President Delcy Rodríguez called Gil a 'living miracle' and described the disaster as 'a natural tragedy on a scale we never imagined'
Tens of thousands remain missing as rescue operations continue with international assistance from Chile and Costa Rica
Rodríguez rejected criticism of slow government response, stating authorities deployed thousands of officials after the quakes

sources.name: BBC World
sources.framing_note: Focused on rescue operation details and survivor story

sources.name: Le Monde
sources.framing_note: Title visible but full article content unavailable

top_stories.headline: China Expands Maritime Claims East of Taiwan, Drawing Australian Concern
bullets: Chinese coast guard ships conducted 'special maritime traffic law-enforcement operations' off Taiwan's east coast in June, inspecting 198 vessels
Beijing claimed the operation was in response to Japan-Philippines talks on maritime boundaries, which China views as involving Chinese waters
Chinese state media reported the operation demonstrated strengthened 'traffic control capabilities' in the area
Australia raised concerns directly with Beijing officials, stating opposition to 'any unilateral action to change the status quo'

sources.name: ABC Australia
sources.framing_note: Emphasized Australian diplomatic response and strategic implications

sources.name: Financial Times
sources.framing_note: Headline indicates coverage but full article behind paywall

top_stories.headline: NATO Summit in Ankara Next Week Amid Transatlantic Tensions Over Defense Spending
bullets: NATO leaders will gather in Ankara on July 7-8, with Europeans seeking to demonstrate increased defense commitments as the US scales back alliance involvement
Arms deals worth tens of billions of dollars are expected to be signed, and leaders will pledge continued weapons funding for Ukraine
Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky will attend a dinner hosted by Turkish President Erdogan, who will also hold bilateral talks with President Trump
Trump criticized NATO on Truth Social Thursday, complaining the US spends money protecting members 'without getting any benefit'

sources.name: The Hindu
sources.framing_note: Balanced coverage of European efforts to demonstrate commitment while noting Trump's criticism

sources.name: The Economist
sources.framing_note: Headline suggests focus on European defense posture as America 'pulls back'

West African floods kill at least 59 in Côte d'Ivoire and 13 in Ghana as torrential rains continue

Amnesty International accuses Sudan's RSF of crimes against humanity and ethnic cleansing in El Fasher campaign
